step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the selection of Option A. In bicuspidization, it involves dividing an existing mandibular molar into two bicuspids through careful separation of its mesial and distal roots along with their corresponding crown segments. This method ensures that each resulting cusp has both a complete root system and crown structure necessary for functional integrity post-procedure. Therefore, my final answer remains A. Analysis confirms the necessity of retaining full crown-root association during such dental surgeries.
